questions.question: Explain the relationship between a Region and an Availability Zone in public cloud architecture. Why is this design important?
expectedPoints: A Region is a large geographical area.
A Region contains multiple isolated locations called Availability Zones (AZs).
Each AZ is a physically separate data center with independent infrastructure.
The design is crucial for building fault-tolerant applications; a failure in one AZ does not affect others in the same Region.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the strategic focus and key strengths of Amazon Web Services (AWS), Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). Provide examples of flagship services that demonstrate these strengths.
expectedPoints: AWS: Market leader with the broadest range of services. Strength in maturity and enterprise adoption. Example: EC2 (compute), S3 (storage).
Azure: Strength in hybrid cloud integration and deep ties to the Microsoft ecosystem (Windows Server, Active Directory, .NET). Example: Azure Arc, Azure Virtual Desktop.
GCP: Strength in data analytics, artificial intelligence/machine learning, and container orchestration (Kubernetes). Example: BigQuery (analytics), GKE (Kubernetes).
Discussion of global infrastructure differences (e.g., AWS has most AZs, GCP has a premium network).
Mention of target audience: AWS for broad needs, Azure for Microsoft shops, GCP for data-driven and cloud-native companies.
